question: Rewrite the events as a diary entry from Samuel Pepys, focusing on his preparations for the Queen and observations of court drama involving my Lord and W. Howe, including my Lord's new role.
expected_answer: Diary Entry — This day, my chief occupation was in making ready sundry items to be dispatched to the Queen, who is soon to be brought over to England by my Lord, now lately advanced to the dignity of Embassador by His Majesty. The court is in a state of great agitation; I observed my Lord, usually so composed, lose his temper with W. Howe, the exchange sharp and ill-concealed from those nearby. Such tempers, I think, are but a reflection of the weighty matters at hand, for the Portuguese Embassador himself came to White Hall to take leave of the King, signaling the nearness of the Queen's arrival and the delicate business that attends it. Amidst these preparations and intrigues, I find myself both anxious and eager, for the eyes of many are upon us in these days of high expectation.
rationale: GraphRAG-Bench Novel (Creative Generation) — Xiang et al., ICLR 2026 / arXiv:2506.05690
